commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(photos): route delete to correct endpoint when profile photo is absent

deletePhoto(index) assumed index 0 always maps to the profile photo, but
ProfileModel.allPhotos only prepends profilePhotoUrl when it is non-null.
After the profile photo was deleted, allPhotos[0] became the first
additional photo, yet deletePhoto(0) still called deleteProfilePhoto(),
which returned 400, reverted the optimistic state, and left the photo
permanently stuck (immortal image bug).

Fix: derive isProfilePhoto from whether profilePhotoUrl is actually
present, and compute additionalIndex accordingly, so the correct
endpoint and array offset are used regardless of whether a profile photo
exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CLAUDE.md, fix simplify findings

Add CLAUDE.md with project conventions, architecture, and common
pitfalls to avoid (sed -i, hardcoded counts, duplicated cwd setup,
temp file paths, missing shared constants).

Simplify fixes:
- Extract PLATFORMS_LINUX shared constant, use in dcss.c/supertux.c
- Expose NUM_PLAT_FILTERS in ui.h, replace magic % 4
- Extract source_cwd() helper, deduplicate terminal/graphical launch
- Use mktemp for zip download temp files
- Collapse platform filter bar rendering with PLAT_COLORS array
- Cache plat_total/plat_installed in App, remove per-frame recomputation

cumulative swipe distance, resizes the panel live, and dismisses it

if the swipe is too short.

commit.message:
fix: prevent CLAUDECODE env leak crash loop; add atomic send_reply+mark_processed

scripts/claude-persistent.sh: Strip CLAUDECODE and CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT
from both the shell environment and tmux's global environment before every
Claude launch. These vars leak into tmux on restart and cause "cannot be
launched inside another Claude Code session" crash loops.

src/mcp/inbox_server.py: Add optional message_id param to send_reply tool.
When provided, atomically marks the message processed in the same call
(combines send_reply + mark_processed into one API call). Also improves
mark_processed to auto-send a "Noted." fallback reply instead of a soft
warning when no reply was sent for a human message, and skips the guard
for callback (button press) messages.

fix: use window.location.search instead of useSearchParams for Shopify CSE handoff

Next.js useSearchParams() returns stale/cached values on subsequent
cross-domain navigations, causing the second image load from
cakesandmemories.com to fail. Switch to reading window.location.search
directly and use window.history.replaceState for URL cleanup to bypass
the Next.js router cache entirely.

Fix middleware JWT verification to accept AUTH_SECRET or NEXTAUTH_SECRET

is not set so token validation works regardless of which env var name

commit.message:
Improve prediction popup latency by caching domain settings and removing tabs.get pre-flight (#291)

Two performance bottlenecks were causing ~15-25 ms of avoidable overhead on
every keystroke prediction:

1. `resolveDomainRuntimeSettings` was called on every prediction request,
   performing 5 parallel chrome.storage reads (~5-15 ms) even though domain
   settings virtually never change between keystrokes.
   Fix: introduce `DomainSettingsCache` (500 ms TTL, per-domain key) in
   `MessageRouter`.  The cache is immediately invalidated when
   `CMD_OPTIONS_PAGE_CONFIG_CHANGE` is received so settings changes still
   take effect without waiting for expiry.

2. `BackgroundServiceWorker.runPrediction` called `chrome.tabs.get` before
   every `sendMessage` as a tab-existence pre-flight, adding ~5-10 ms of
   extra IPC latency.  `sendMessage` already throws when the tab/frame is
   gone, so the pre-flight was redundant.
   Fix: call `chrome.tabs.sendMessage` directly and catch the error.

Performance gain per keystroke: ~15-25 ms less background-side overhead.
50 consecutive predictions for the same domain now incur 1 storage read
instead of 250.

Tests added:
- `tests/DomainSettingsCache.test.ts` — 11 unit tests covering cache hit/miss,
  TTL expiry, per-domain isolation, invalidation, and a latency benchmark
  asserting cached requests are ≥5× faster than uncached ones.
- Two regression tests in `tests/background.routing.test.ts`:
  - Verifies `chrome.tabs.get` is NOT called during prediction dispatch.
  - Verifies the domain settings cache is invalidated after a config change
    so the next prediction sees fresh language/settings values.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve LIST and MAP types from ConvertedType-only group nodes (#477)

## Summary

- Adds a `ConvertedType` fallback in the group-node path of
`openColumns` (`column.go:362-375`) so that `deprecated.Map` and
`deprecated.List` are correctly mapped to `&mapType{}` and `&listType{}`
when `LogicalType` is absent
- This fixes files from older parquet writers that only set
`ConvertedType` (the legacy format) — the leaf-node path
(`schemaElementTypeOf`) already handled this correctly, but the
group-node path did not
- Adds `TestGroupConvertedTypeFallback` with sub-tests for `list`,
`map`, and `map_key_value` converted types

passes
- [x] `go test -run TestSchemaRoundTrip -v .` — no regressions
- [x] `go test -run TestOpenFile -v .` — testdata files still load
correctly

Fixes #57

feat: YouTube extractor with three-tier fallback (v0.2.0)

Three-tier architecture for reliable YouTube downloads:
- Tier 1: kkdai/youtube (pure Go, fast metadata + direct URLs)
- Tier 2: go-ytdlp (subprocess, handles POT/anti-bot measures)
- Tier 3: ox-browser (CF bypass, page fetch + player response parsing)

Core improvements:
- Add LocalPath field to Media (skip download when extractor provides file)
- Add AudioURL field to Media (separate DASH audio streams)
- Add MergeAudioVideo() for DASH video+audio muxing via ffmpeg
- Update Processor to handle LocalPath and DASH merge

9 new tests (38 total), 0 lint issues.

commit.message:
ci: build-and-push 워크플로우에 fetch_cli.py 실행 옵션을 추가합니다 (#891)

## Description

- Dockerfile Stage 1의 `fetch_cli.py` 인자를 `ARG FETCH_ARGS`로 파라미터화합니다.
- `workflow_dispatch` 수동 실행 시 fetch 방식을 선택할 수 있습니다.
- `--recent --attachments` (기본값) — `fetch_state.yaml`의 마지막 수집 시점 이후 변경
페이지만 수집
  - `--remote --attachments` — 전체 페이지 트리를 API로 다시 수집
- `push` / `pull_request` 자동 트리거 시에는 기본값 `--recent --attachments`가
적용됩니다.
- `--build-arg "FETCH_ARGS=..."` 로 Dockerfile에 전달되어 이미지에 결과가 반영됩니다.

## Added/updated tests?
- [x] No, and this is why: CI 워크플로우 및 Dockerfile 설정 변경으로 별도 테스트 불필요

## Additional notes
- Confluence 공간(QM)은 공개 접근 가능하므로 인증 없이 API 호출이 성공합니다.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add GPU Lesson 38 — Indirect Drawing (#208)

* Add GPU Lesson 38 — Indirect Drawing

Introduces GPU-driven rendering with SDL_DrawGPUIndexedPrimitivesIndirect.
A compute shader performs frustum culling and writes indirect draw commands,
so the CPU never decides what to draw.

New APIs covered:
- SDL_DrawGPUIndexedPrimitivesIndirect — GPU reads draw args from a buffer
- SDL_GPUIndexedIndirectDrawCommand — struct for indirect draw parameters
- Compute-to-render buffer synchronization via SDL GPU auto-barriers

Architecture:
- Compute pass: frustum cull 200 objects, write indirect buffer + visibility
- Shadow pass: truck (regular) + boxes (indirect draw)
- Main pass: split-screen with main camera (indirect) and debug camera
  (all objects color-coded green/red by visibility)

Files added:
- lessons/gpu/38-indirect-drawing/main.c — 3570-line lesson implementation
- lessons/gpu/38-indirect-drawing/README.md — comprehensive walkthrough
- lessons/gpu/38-indirect-drawing/shaders/ — 13 HLSL shaders (compute + graphics)
- lessons/gpu/38-indirect-drawing/assets/ — screenshot + 6 matplotlib diagrams
- .claude/skills/indirect-drawing/SKILL.md — reusable indirect drawing skill
- scripts/forge_diagrams/gpu/lesson_38.py — 6 diagram functions

Also updates root CMakeLists.txt, PLAN.md, GPU README, and diagram
infrastructure to integrate the new lesson.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #208 feedback: math block formatting and diagram label

- Split single-line $$...$$ math blocks into 3-line format (lines 187, 451)
  per markdownlint-cli2 rules
- Fix sphere-frustum test label: "Pass: d > -r" → "Pass: d >= -r" to include
  the tangent boundary case matching the shader's cull rule (d < -r)
- Regenerate sphere_frustum_test.png with corrected label

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #208 feedback round 2: case bounds, ASCII diagram, labels

- Make sphere/plane case bullets exhaustive with >= and range notation
  to match shader's d < -radius cull condition
- Replace ASCII flow diagram with numbered steps (repo prefers generated
  visuals or plain text over ASCII art)
- Relabel pipeline diagram arrows to clarify buffer flow: compute produces
  indirect + visibility, shadow pass produces shadow map for main render
- Regenerate compute_to_render_pipeline.png with updated labels

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
